target.title: feat(session): add chat-channel session switching with LRU history retention
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: Chat channels did not support listing or switching between prior sessions under the same SessionKey, and follow-up fixes were needed to preserve switchable transcripts until LRU eviction and restore per-session usage/compaction snapshots correctly.
- Why it matters: Without in-chat session switching, users cannot navigate past conversations from Feishu/DingTalk/Telegram/Discord-style chat surfaces; incorrect transcript/archive or snapshot behavior would also make `/session back`, `/sessions`, and `/status`-adjacent state feel unreliable.
- What changed: Added `/sessions`, `/session <number>`, `/session <sessionId>`, and `/session back` for chat channels; introduced per-SessionKey LRU `sessionHistory`; preserved switchable transcripts until history eviction; restored token/context/compaction snapshots on switch; documented matching/order/retention behavior; cleaned up reset semantics so `session.historyLimit=0` truly disables switchable history.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This PR does not change SessionKey routing, does not add cross-SessionKey switching, does not add named sessions/UI picker flows, and does not change channel plugin-specific transport behavior.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #9959
- Related #28168

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

List user-visible changes (including defaults/config).  
If none, write `None`.

- Added `/sessions` to list switchable sessions for the current chat SessionKey.
- Added `/session <number>`, `/session <sessionId>`, and `/session back` for in-chat session switching.
- Current session is included in `/sessions`, marked as `[current]`.
- `/sessions` now shows compact single-line preview rows with session ID prefix and truncated preview text.
- Session switching now restores stored token/context/compaction snapshots for the target session.
- `session.historyLimit` now consistently supports `0` as “disable switchable session history”.
- Transcripts for sessions still in history remain switchable; transcripts are archived when evicted from the LRU history queue.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local OpenClaw dev runtime / source tree
- Model/provider: N/A for core session-switch logic
- Integration/channel (if any): chat-channel command flow; manually validated in a real chat session
- Relevant config (redacted):
  - `session.historyLimit: 5` (default path)
  - `session.historyLimit: 0` (disable-history regression path)

### Steps

1. Start a chat conversation and create multiple sessions with `/new`.
2. Run `/sessions`, then switch via `/session <number>` or `/session back`.
3. Continue creating sessions until the history limit is exceeded.

### Expected

- `/sessions` lists current + recent switchable sessions in LRU order.
- Switching restores the target session’s stored metadata/snapshots.
- Sessions still present in history remain switchable.
- The oldest session is archived only after LRU eviction.
- With `session.historyLimit=0`, switchable history is disabled and the previous session is archived immediately on reset.

### Actual

- Behavior matches the expected results above in targeted tests and manual validation.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - `/sessions` output in chat
  - `/session back` restores the previous session
  - switching to a still-retained historical session restores expected context/state
  - LRU eviction archives only the oldest evicted session
  - daily reset path preserves switchable history
  - `session.historyLimit=0` disables switchable history and archives immediately on reset
- Edge cases checked:
  - history boundary behavior at the configured limit
  - selecting current session is a no-op
  - unique-prefix SessionId selection behavior
  - stale/daily-reset session rollover
- What you did **not** verify:
  - every individual chat provider/plugin surface
  - cross-SessionKey switching (out of scope)
  - any UI/card-based session picker (out of scope)

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly:
  - revert this PR, or set `session.historyLimit=0` to disable switchable session history
- Files/config to restore:
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session.ts`
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts`
  - `src/config/sessions/types.ts`
  - `src/config/types.base.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for:
  - `/sessions` shows stale/incorrect ordering
  - switched sessions lose expected token/context/compaction snapshot state
  - transcripts are archived too early while still present in history
  - `session.historyLimit=0` does not disable switchable history

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

- Risk: LRU eviction/archive timing could regress and archive a session that should still remain switchable.
  - Mitigation: Added/updated targeted tests covering retained-history vs evicted-history behavior.

- Risk: Snapshot restore could leave token/context/compaction state inconsistent after switching.
  - Mitigation: Added lifecycle tests covering restore and missing-metadata fallback behavior.

- Risk: Docs could drift from implementation again as follow-up fixes land.
  - Mitigation: Updated `docs/design/chat-session-switch.md` to match the current implementation semantics in this PR.

## AI Assistance

AI-assisted: Yes

I used an AI assistant for code review, doc cleanup, and targeted test updates. I manually reviewed the final diff, understood the changes, and verified the session-switch flows myself.

target.title: fix(nextcloud-talk): parse rich object file shares from webhook payload
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #29152

When a user shares a file or image in Nextcloud Talk, the bot webhook receives the message but the NC Talk plugin was not parsing the rich object parameters — so the agent never saw the attachment.

NC Talk encodes file shares as rich object messages where `object.content` is a JSON string:
```json
{"message":"{file}","parameters":{"file":{"type":"file","id":"12345","name":"IMG_123.jpg","path":"Talk/IMG_123.jpg","link":"https://cloud.example.com/f/12345","mimetype":"image/jpeg","preview-available":"yes"}}}
```

Previously `payloadToInboundMessage()` passed this raw JSON string as `text`, leaving `mediaUrls` unpopulated.

## Changes

### `monitor.ts`
- Adds `parseRichObjectContent()` helper that detects JSON-encoded rich object payloads
- Extracts file links into `mediaUrls` (https/http only — rejects `file://`, `javascript:`, etc.)
- Resolves `{placeholder}` names for human-readable `displayText` (covers file shares, mentions, deck cards, polls, etc.)
- Uses `replaceAll` for placeholder substitution to handle duplicate occurrences
- Falls back to raw `content` for non-rich-object text — no behaviour change for regular messages

### `types.ts`
- Adds `mediaUrls?: string[]` to `NextcloudTalkInboundMessage`

### `inbound.ts`
- Passes `MediaUrls` into `finalizeInboundContext` (verified: already consumed natively as `string[]`)
- Relaxes the empty-body guard to allow media-only messages through (previously silently dropped)

## Tests

- `monitor.attachment.test.ts` — 7 unit tests: file share, text passthrough, malformed JSON fallback, name fallback, mention resolution, `file://` URL rejection, multi-file
- `monitor.live-integration.test.ts` — 5 integration tests using real HMAC-signed payloads against the webhook server

All 12 tests pass. TypeScript compiles clean (`tsc --noEmit`).

## Before / After

**Before:** Sharing a file in NC Talk → agent receives raw JSON string, never sees the attachment.

**After:** Sharing a file → agent receives `text = "filename.jpg"` and `mediaUrls = ["https://..."]` — can immediately process the image via vision without any manual workaround.

target.title: fix: preserve logical symlinked session paths
target.body:
## Summary
- keep realpath-based safety checks for session files, but return and persist logical session paths under the caller-visible sessions directory
- normalize legacy stored real paths back to the logical symlinked path so `openclaw doctor` does not report false orphan transcripts
- add regression coverage for symlinked session dirs, sibling fallback handling, and doctor orphan detection

## Reproduction
Before this change, a symlinked `~/.openclaw` caused `resolveSessionFilePath()` to canonicalize `sessionFile` values into the symlink target. `openclaw doctor` scans the logical sessions directory, so those stored real paths were misclassified as orphaned transcripts.

## Validation
- reproduced the mismatch with a temporary symlinked state dir before the fix
- reran the same harness after the fix and verified both logical and legacy stored real paths resolve back to the logical transcript path
- added regression coverage in `src/config/sessions/sessions.test.ts` and `src/commands/doctor-state-integrity.test.ts`

## AI Assistance
- AI-assisted: yes (implemented with Codex plus manual verification and follow-up fixes)
- Testing degree: targeted/source-level validation plus focused regression tests in the changed modules; full local `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` was not available in the initial sandbox, so broader validation is currently delegated to repository CI

Fixes #49708

target.title: docs/types: clarify that omitting accountId in bindings matches default account only, not all accounts
target.body:
## Summary

- **Fix**: Auto-set `accountId: "*"` when a channel has multiple accounts configured and no explicit accountId is provided during `openclaw agents add` / `openclaw agents bind`
- **Docs**: Add JSDoc to `AgentBindingMatch.accountId` documenting the three behaviors (omitted → default only, `"*"` → all, string → specific)
- **Docs**: Replace easy-to-miss plain text note in `multi-agent.md` with a prominent `[!WARNING]` callout
- **Docs**: Add `--bind` format reference table and `agents add --bind` examples to CLI docs
- **Docs**: Update binding examples to include `accountId: "*"` as the safe default

## Problem

When configuring multi-agent routing with `bindings` in `openclaw.json`, omitting the `accountId` field silently matches **only the default account** rather than all accounts. This is counter-intuitive and causes hard-to-debug routing failures when multiple channel accounts are configured (e.g. WhatsApp `personal` + `biz`, Telegram `default` + `alerts`).

### Scenario

A user configures two accounts on a channel and sets up bindings to route specific groups to specific agents:

```json
{
  "channels": {
    "telegram": {
      "accounts": {
        "default": { "botToken": "123456:ABC..." },
        "alerts": { "botToken": "987654:XYZ..." }
      }
    }
  },
  "bindings": [
    {
      "match": {
        "channel": "telegram",
        "peer": { "kind": "group", "id": "-100123456789" }
      },
      "agentId": "work"
    }
  ]
}
```

**Expected**: The binding routes messages from the group to the `work` agent regardless of which account receives the message.

**Actual**:
- Messages via `telegram[default]` → ✅ correctly dispatched to `work` agent
- Messages via `telegram[alerts]` → ❌ silently falls back to `main` agent

### Root cause (in `src/routing/resolve-route.ts`)

`resolveAccountPatternKey()` converts an empty `accountPattern` (from an omitted `accountId`) to the literal string `"default"`:

```typescript
function resolveAccountPatternKey(accountPattern: string): string {
  if (!accountPattern.trim()) {
    return DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID;  // → "default"
  }
  return normalizeAccountId(accountPattern);
}
```

During `buildEvaluatedBindingsByChannel()`, bindings without `accountId` are indexed into the `byAccount.get("default")` bucket. Only bindings with `accountId: "*"` enter `byAnyAccount`.

At lookup time in `getEvaluatedBindingsForChannelAccount()`:

```typescript
const accountScoped = channelBindings?.byAccount.get(accountId) ?? [];
const anyAccount = channelBindings?.byAnyAccount ?? [];
```

When a message arrives from a non-default account, the system queries `byAccount.get("alerts")` — which is empty. `byAnyAccount` is also empty. No binding matches → fallback to default agent.

### Why this is confusing

1. Most users expect "field omitted" = "match any / don't care", not "match only default"
2. The `openclaw agents add` wizard does **not** prompt for `accountId` when creating bindings, even when multiple accounts are configured
3. Many doc examples showed bindings without `accountId`, implying this is the normal pattern
4. The TypeScript type `AgentBindingMatch.accountId` had no JSDoc explaining the default behavior
5. The CLI docs had no `--bind` format reference or examples showing the `:*` wildcard syntax

### Related issues

- #26066 — Feishu accountId binding not working
- #17817 — Telegram multi-account: accountId in bindings ignored
- #26421 — Feishu Channel Binding Routing Not Working
- #26199 — Channel agentId 配置未生效

## Changes

### 1. `src/commands/agents.bindings.ts` — CLI behavior fix (core fix)

In `resolveBindingAccountId()`, when a channel has **multiple accounts** configured and no explicit `accountId` is provided (by the user or plugin), the function now returns `"*"` instead of `undefined`. This ensures that `openclaw agents add` and `openclaw agents bind` automatically create bindings that match all accounts on multi-account channels.

The fix is safe and backward-compatible:
- If user explicitly specifies accountId (e.g. `--bind telegram:alerts`), that is used (no change)
- If plugin provides its own `resolveBindingAccountId` hook, that is used (no change)
- If plugin has `forceAccountBinding`, the default account is used (no change)
- **NEW**: If channel has >1 accounts, `"*"` is returned (fixes the issue)
- If channel has only 1 account, `undefined` is returned (no change)

### 2. `src/config/types.agents.ts` — Type annotation

Added JSDoc to `AgentBindingMatch.accountId` explicitly documenting all three behaviors (omitted → default only, `"*"` → wildcard, string → specific account).

### 3. `docs/concepts/multi-agent.md` — Core routing docs

- Replaced the easy-to-miss plain text "Important account-scope detail" with a prominent `> [!WARNING]` callout block
- Updated "WhatsApp daily chat + Telegram deep work" example bindings to include `accountId: "*"`
- Updated "same channel, one peer to Opus" example bindings to include `accountId: "*"`
- Added `> [!TIP]` explaining that `"*"` is always safe even for single-account setups

### 4. `docs/cli/agents.md` — CLI reference

- Added `> [!WARNING]` callout to the "Binding scope behavior" section
- Added `--bind` format reference table (`:*`, `:<account>`, bare channel)
- Added example showing `agents add --bind` at creation time
- Added wildcard and specific-account examples to the bind examples section

## Test plan

- [ ] Run `openclaw agents add` with a channel that has multiple accounts — verify the created binding includes `accountId: "*"`
- [ ] Run `openclaw agents bind --bind telegram` with multiple telegram accounts — verify `accountId: "*"` is set
- [ ] Run `openclaw agents bind --bind telegram:alerts` — verify explicit accountId is preserved (not overridden to `"*"`)
- [ ] Run `openclaw agents add` with a single-account channel — verify no `accountId` is added (same behavior as before)
- [ ] Verify JSDoc renders correctly in IDE hover tooltips
- [ ] Verify `[!WARNING]` / `[!TIP]` callouts render properly on docs.openclaw.ai

Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/whatsapp/whatsapp-live.runtime.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/shared/live-gateway.runtime.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/monitor-inbox.behavior.test.ts`

- Testbox-through-Crabbox `tbx_01krx9ne1a4egr0qxpp22chkf5`: scoped `pnpm check:changed -- <touched paths>`, exit 0
- Testbox-through-Crabbox `tbx_01krxagz91ep6d1ca29qx5b00q`: scoped `pnpm check:changed -- <touched paths>`, exit 0

Live evidence
- `26027068186` at `fdc2d0e9`: WhatsApp job passed; RTT `7532ms`; trace isolated ~`5141ms` in agent/model before outbound send.
- `26027596921` at `74c4bb73`: WhatsApp job passed; RTT `7930ms`; trace isolated ~`5890ms` in agent/model before outbound send.
- `26028278115` at `9ef7fbe1`: WhatsApp job passed; RTT `10393ms`; trace isolated ~`7129ms` in agent/model before outbound send.

Behavior addressed: WhatsApp live QA canary artifacts now separate channel/provider cost from agent/model cost, capture complete heap snapshots, and avoid unnecessary setup/group hydration in the DM-only path.
Real environment tested: live WhatsApp Convex QA on GitHub Actions, plus Blacksmith Testbox-through-Crabbox changed gates.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm openclaw qa whatsapp --provider-mode live-frontier --model openai/gpt-5.5 --alt-model openai/gpt-5.5 --scenario whatsapp-canary` through `qa-live-transports-convex.yml`.

Observed result after fix: this branch improves observability and removes some setup work, but it does not yet reduce the live canary into the target range; current dominant cost is agent/model pre-outbound latency.
What was not tested: full workflow green is not claimed; the exact workflow runs may show unrelated lane failures after the WhatsApp job/artifact succeeded.

## Summary
- Render fenced ` ```mermaid ` blocks in Control UI webchat with lazy-loaded Mermaid and dedicated chat styling.
- Keep Mermaid source blocks collapsible and copy-friendly in the transcript UI.
- Harden Mermaid rendering:
  - case-insensitive language fence detection (`mermaid` / `Mermaid`)
  - sanitize rendered SVG before insertion
  - guard against transient module-load/render failures

## Screenshots
Rendered Mermaid diagram in Control UI webchat:

![Mermaid diagram rendered in Control UI webchat](https://files.catbox.moe/efp3l7.png)

## CI/compat follow-ups included in this PR
- Stabilize channel contract tests by moving some imports from module scope into test scope to avoid eager side effects.
- Update contract registry imports to use explicit extension internals where runtime APIs no longer expose these helpers.
- Make bundle MCP path assertions robust across symlink/realpath environments (Windows/CI path normalization).

- Contract tests: inbound/registry contract paths updated for current plugin boundaries.
- CI: full repository checks.

target.title: fix(gateway): include MCP tools in effective inventory
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the Agent Tools dashboard / `tools.effective` inventory originally reported only static OpenClaw tools, so configured bundled MCP tools could be usable at runtime but absent from the session-effective inventory.
- Solution: split the Gateway surface so `tools.effective` is a read-only peek projection with explicit MCP notices, and add `tools.effective.refresh` as an `operator.write` method that performs live MCP discovery/materialization on demand.
- What changed: effective inventory projection supports `source: "mcp"`; session MCP runtimes expose peek-only catalog metadata; the Gateway read path no longer cold-starts MCP transports; the refresh path materializes MCP tools through the existing runtime/materializer/final-policy path; Control UI adds an explicit **Refresh Available Tools** action; SDK/docs/protocol/tests cover the new split.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no provider transport changes, no Tool Search/Code Mode raw provider-body changes, no auto-reply `/tools` command changes, no install-package-dir changes, no broad shared-runtime refactor, and no claim that #80909 or the reporter-specific SSE raw provider-body path is fixed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #: N/A (intentionally not closing #76724; this keeps the reporter-specific SSE/runtime-provider-body path out of scope)
- Related / Refs #76724
- Related #70876
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

#76724 reports MCP handshake success while the Agent Tools dashboard stays stuck at the pre-MCP tool count and does not list MCP tools. Runtime code can materialize bundled MCP tools for model runs, but the dashboard inventory used a separate static inventory path. The initial patch made `tools.effective` perform live MCP materialization; after review of the read/security boundary, this update keeps `tools.effective` read-only and moves live discovery behind an explicit refresh method.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `tools.effective` should not cold-start/list MCP transports from a read-scoped dashboard load, while the dashboard must still have an explicit path to discover configured MCP tools and then show the warm catalog.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw Gateway from this branch at `3c7a4e67b6`, QA mock OpenAI provider, real stdio MCP server created by the proof harness, Tool Search disabled, Code Mode disabled.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
OPENCLAW_REPO_ROOT=/root/repos/openclaw-mcp-tools-effective \
OPENCLAW_LOCAL_HEAD=3c7a4e67b6 \
OPENCLAW_UPSTREAM_COMMIT=110042d840 \
OPENCLAW_RPC_PROOF_SUMMARY=/tmp/openclaw-mcp-tools-effective-d-proof-summary.json \
node --import tsx /tmp/openclaw-mcp-tools-effective-d-proof.ts
```

- Evidence after fix:

Copied live Gateway RPC harness output from this branch:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "proof": "D read/refresh split: cold read peek-only, explicit refresh materializes MCP, warm read peeks catalog",
  "upstreamCommit": "110042d840",
  "localHead": "3c7a4e67b6",
  "cold": {
    "noticeIds": ["mcp-not-yet-connected"],
    "containsMcpTarget": false,
    "runtimeCreated": false,
    "groupSummaries": [
      { "id": "core", "source": "core", "toolCount": 18 },
      { "id": "plugin", "source": "plugin", "toolCount": 2 }
    ]
  },
  "refreshed": {
    "noticeIds": [],
    "containsMcpTarget": true,
    "matchingIds": ["reproProbe__probe_tool"],
    "runtimeCreated": true,
    "catalogToolNames": ["probe_tool"],
    "groupSummaries": [
      { "id": "core", "source": "core", "toolCount": 18 },
      { "id": "plugin", "source": "plugin", "toolCount": 2 },
      { "id": "mcp", "source": "mcp", "toolCount": 1, "sampleIds": ["reproProbe__probe_tool"] }
    ]
  },
  "warm": {
    "noticeIds": [],
    "containsMcpTarget": true,
    "matchingIds": ["reproProbe__probe_tool"],
    "groupSummaries": [
      { "id": "core", "source": "core", "toolCount": 18 },
      { "id": "plugin", "source": "plugin", "toolCount": 2 },
      { "id": "mcp", "source": "mcp", "toolCount": 1, "sampleIds": ["reproProbe__probe_tool"] }
    ]
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: a cold `tools.effective` read returns only core/plugin groups plus `mcp-not-yet-connected` and does not create a session MCP runtime; explicit `tools.effective.refresh` materializes the configured stdio MCP server and returns `reproProbe__probe_tool`; the next `tools.effective` read shows the warm MCP group via peek without another cold materialization.
- What was not tested: the reporter's original Flask SSE server and release version `v2026.5.2`; Tool Search / Code Mode raw provider-body behavior; #80909; full cross-platform/browser E2E. This PR is scoped to the Gateway/dashboard effective-inventory boundary.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): pre-patch proof on current main returned 20 tools and omitted `reproProbe__probe_tool` from `tools.effective` while the runtime provider path could include it.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `tools.effective` called the static `resolveEffectiveToolInventory()` resolver, which builds from `createOpenClawCodingTools()` only. Bundled MCP tools are appended later in the model-run path through the session MCP runtime/materializer, so the dashboard inventory and runtime provider path diverged.
- Boundary correction in this update: live MCP discovery/listing can start configured stdio/remote transports, so it now lives behind explicit `operator.write` `tools.effective.refresh`; the read-scoped method only peeks at an existing matching-fingerprint runtime catalog and otherwise returns a notice.
- Missing detection / guardrail: tests covered core/plugin/channel grouping but did not cover bundled MCP catalog projection, cold-read side effects, refresh materialization, or stale MCP config fingerprints through the `tools.effective` handler/RPC path.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] End-to-end-style Gateway RPC proof
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/tools-effective.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts`
  - `packages/sdk/src/index.test.ts`
  - `packages/sdk/src/index.e2e.test.ts`
  - `ui/src/ui/controllers/agents.test.ts`
  - `ui/src/ui/views/agents-panels-tools-skills.browser.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: cold `tools.effective` does not create/list MCP and returns notices; warm read projects an existing same-fingerprint catalog; fingerprint mismatch returns refresh-needed notice instead of stale MCP tools; explicit `tools.effective.refresh` materializes MCP tools and applies final effective policy; UI uses read on normal load and refresh on explicit action.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug and security-boundary concern live at the seam between static inventory, session MCP runtime, Gateway method scope, SDK, and UI refresh behavior.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none found for bundled MCP in `tools-effective` / `tools-effective-inventory` before this patch.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

The Agent Tools panel now loads without implicitly starting configured MCP transports. If MCP servers are configured but not connected/listed yet, the panel shows an explanatory notice and a **Refresh Available Tools** action. Triggering refresh performs live discovery and lets subsequent reads show the warm MCP tools.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before this PR:
[tools.effective] -> [static createOpenClawCodingTools inventory] -> [core/plugin/channel only]

Updated design:
[tools.effective]         -> [static inventory + peek existing MCP catalog] -> [core/plugin/channel + notices or warm mcp]
[tools.effective.refresh] -> [session MCP runtime materialize] -> [final bundled-tool policy] -> [core/plugin/channel + mcp]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No new capability beyond existing configured MCP materialization; yes, a new explicit Gateway method is added.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? Yes, only from explicit `tools.effective.refresh` or existing run-lifecycle MCP materialization, not from cold `tools.effective` reads.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes, refresh can start/list configured MCP transports; it does not invoke MCP tools.
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: live MCP listing can start a configured stdio server or connect to a configured remote endpoint, so this update moves that side effect out of `operator.read` and into explicit `operator.write` `tools.effective.refresh`. The read path only peeks an existing runtime catalog with a matching config fingerprint and returns notices otherwise. Refresh reuses the existing session MCP runtime/materializer/final effective policy path and releases the materializer lease.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux local dev environment
- Runtime/container: local Node via `node --import tsx`
- Model/provider: QA mock OpenAI provider, `mock-openai/gpt-5.5`
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway WebSocket RPC `sessions.create`, `tools.effective`, `tools.effective.refresh`, then `tools.effective`
- Relevant config (redacted): `tools.profile: "coding"`, `tools.toolSearch: false`, `tools.codeMode.enabled: false`, `mcp.servers.reproProbe` stdio server command `node <tmp>/dummy-mcp-server.mjs`

### Steps

1. Start QA mock provider and local Gateway from this branch.
2. Create a session with `sessions.create`.
3. Call cold `tools.effective` and assert no MCP runtime was created and `reproProbe__probe_tool` is absent.
4. Call `tools.effective.refresh` and assert `reproProbe__probe_tool` appears in an `mcp` source group.
5. Call warm `tools.effective` and assert the MCP group is projected via peek.

### Expected

- Cold read: no MCP cold-start/listing; notice returned.
- Refresh: MCP listed/materialized through explicit write-scoped method.
- Warm read: MCP group visible through existing catalog peek.

### Actual

- Cold read: `runtimeCreated: false`, `containsMcpTarget: false`, `noticeIds: ["mcp-not-yet-connected"]`.
- Refresh: `runtimeCreated: true`, `matchingIds: ["reproProbe__probe_tool"]`, `catalogToolNames: ["probe_tool"]`.
- Warm read: `containsMcpTarget: true`, no stale not-connected notice.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - `git merge-tree --write-tree upstream/main HEAD` → exit 0
  - `git diff --exit-code $(git merge-base upstream/main HEAD)..HEAD -- CHANGELOG.md` → no contributor-authored changelog entry remains
  - `pnpm check:test-types` → passed
  - `pnpm lint --threads=8` → passed
  - `pnpm protocol:check` → passed
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/tools-effective.test.ts src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-tools.materialize.test.ts src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-tools.request-boundary.test.ts packages/sdk/src/index.test.ts` → 9 files / 110 tests passed
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts packages/sdk/src/index.e2e.test.ts` → 1 file passed; 4 passed / 1 skipped
  - `cd ui && pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts src/ui/controllers/agents.test.ts src/ui/views/agents-panels-tools-skills.browser.test.ts src/ui/views/agents.test.ts` → 3 files / 28 tests passed
  - real Gateway RPC proof with real stdio MCP server verifies cold read / refresh / warm read behavior
- Edge cases checked:
  - final effective policy still filters materialized MCP tools
  - stale MCP config fingerprints produce refresh-needed behavior instead of serving stale MCP tools
  - read path does not call `getOrCreateSessionMcpRuntime()` on cold load
  - UI notice rendering and explicit refresh action
  - SDK helper calls `tools.effective.refresh`
- What you did **not** verify:
  - reporter's exact Flask SSE MCP server / v2026.5.2 setup
  - Tool Search / Code Mode raw provider-body behavior (out of scope)
  - #80909 raw provider-body behavior (out of scope)
  - full cross-platform/browser E2E

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: users may expect MCP tools to appear immediately on panel load.
  - Mitigation: the panel now displays MCP notices and an explicit refresh action; warm catalogs still appear in read-only inventory.
- Risk: refresh can start configured MCP transports.
  - Mitigation: refresh is explicit and registered as `operator.write`; cold `operator.read` inventory no longer starts or lists MCP transports.
- Risk: dashboard/policy projection may still differ from a live inbound-message run when the UI lacks sender identity.
  - Mitigation: do not accept caller-supplied auth/delivery context; use server-derived session fields and final policy only.
- Risk: overlap with #70876.
  - Mitigation: this PR is narrower and avoids unrelated auto-reply/install-package-dir changes; related work is explicitly linked.

target.title: fix(googlechat): use agent identity.name in typing indicator fallback chain
target.body:
## Problem

Closes #49350

When a Google Chat agent has `identity.name` configured but no direct `agent.name`, the typing indicator was falling back to the hardcoded `"OpenClaw is typing..."` string instead of the configured identity name.

## Root Cause

`resolveBotDisplayName` in `extensions/googlechat/src/monitor.ts` has a 3-step fallback chain:
1. Account config name (`account.config.name`)
2. Agent name (`agent.name`)
3. ❌ Hardcoded `"OpenClaw"`

It was missing a check for `agent.identity?.name`, which is the standard way users configure bot identity in OpenClaw (`IdentityConfig.name`).

## Fix

Add `agent.identity?.name` as a third fallback before the hardcoded `"OpenClaw"` string.

**Updated fallback chain:**
1. Account config name (`account.config.name`)
2. Agent name (`agent.name`)
3. ✅ Agent identity name (`agent.identity?.name`) ← **new**
4. `"OpenClaw"` as last resort

## Example

Config:
```json
{
  "agents": {
    "list": [{
      "id": "main",
      "identity": { "name": "Alvin" }
    }]
  }
}
```

**Before:** Shows `_OpenClaw is typing..._`
**After:** Shows `_Alvin is typing..._`

## Changes

- `extensions/googlechat/src/monitor.ts`: Added `agent?.identity?.name` check in `resolveBotDisplayName` (5 lines)
- Updated JSDoc comment to reflect the 4-step fallback chain

target.title: feat(auth): support and document --profile-id in models auth login
target.body:
## Summary

- add `--profile-id` support to `openclaw models auth login` for single-profile auth flows
- persist the requested profile id when the selected auth method returns exactly one auth profile
- add focused coverage for the profile-id remapping behavior
- document the named-profile login flow for Codex OAuth and related auth-order/session-reset usage

## Why

The docs and installed CLI already point users toward named auth profiles for multi-account setups, especially for `openai-codex`. This PR makes the source repo match that behavior and documents the flow end-to-end.

## Notes

- `--profile-id` intentionally errors when an auth method returns multiple profiles, so we don't silently rename ambiguous results.
- Docs also fix the `/model <alias-or-id>@<profileId>` placeholder notation for consistency.

target.title: Models: add configurable API request delay (#49077)
target.body:
## Summary
- Adds `models.requestDelayMs` to enforce a minimum delay between consecutive **model API request starts** per provider id (shared across sessions).
- Adds per-provider override `models.providers.<id>.requestDelayMs` for stricter rate-limited providers (e.g. GLM).
- Applies the delay to both embedded agent runs and compaction so background summarization doesn’t burst the provider.

## Config
- `models.requestDelayMs`: number (ms), `0..120000`, default `0` (disabled)
- `models.providers.<id>.requestDelayMs`: number (ms), `0..120000`, overrides the global default for that provider

## Docs
- Updates `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md`

## Test plan
- `pnpm install`
- `pnpm test -- src/agents/api-request-delay.test.ts`

target.title: fix(sessions): clean expired archive artifacts during maintenance
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: enforced session maintenance only cleaned archived deleted transcripts when the same save also archived a live-session removal, so stale `.deleted.*` artifacts could survive indefinitely in otherwise healthy stores.
- Why it matters: session directories keep accumulating expired deleted/reset artifacts even though the maintenance docs describe retention-based cleanup.
- What changed: always run deleted/reset archive cleanup against the store directory during enforced maintenance, and broaden archive timestamp parsing so older filename variants are still eligible for retention cleanup.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not change live-session retention policy, lock timeouts, or add new maintenance knobs.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #22506
- Related #48950

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Expired `.deleted.*` and `.reset.*` session archive artifacts are now cleaned up more reliably during enforced session maintenance, including legacy timestamp filename variants.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Ubuntu 24.04
- Runtime/container: Node 22 source checkout
- Model/provider: n/a
- Integration/channel (if any): n/a
- Relevant config (redacted): session maintenance `mode: enforce`

### Steps

1. Create a sessions store with enforced maintenance enabled.
2. Place an expired `.deleted.*` transcript and a fresh `.deleted.*` transcript in the store directory.
3. Save the session store without pruning any live sessions.

### Expected

- The expired archive is removed and the fresh archive remains.

### Actual

- Before this change, the expired archive could remain because cleanup only ran when the same save had already archived a deleted live session.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: ran a direct maintenance save against a temp sessions directory and confirmed an expired `.deleted.*` artifact was pruned while a recent artifact remained; verified legacy archive filename variants like `2026-02-23T12-34-56.000+00-00` and `20260223-123456` parse into retention timestamps.
- Edge cases checked: cleanup still runs when no live sessions were pruned, and legacy timestamp formats remain eligible for cleanup.
- What you did **not** verify: a long-lived production directory with thousands of archived files.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert this commit; maintenance returns to the previous archive-cleanup behavior.
- Files/config to restore: `src/config/sessions/store.ts`, `src/config/sessions/artifacts.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: unexpected deletion of recent session archives or retention cleanup skipping legacy-named artifacts.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: broader timestamp parsing could classify an unexpected filename as an archive artifact.
  - Mitigation: parsing stays limited to the existing `.bak`, `.deleted`, and `.reset` suffix patterns and adds targeted tests for the accepted legacy formats.

target.title: add doctor coverage for stale gateway locks
target.body:
AI-assisted: Codex

Fixes #49036.

## Summary
This change teaches `openclaw doctor` to inspect the gateway lock file, report stale lock ownership, and repair stale gateway locks with `--fix`.

## Root Cause
The CLI already had doctor coverage for stale session locks, but the gateway lock had no matching doctor entry point even though the lock subsystem already encoded enough state to detect dead owners and stale files. When the gateway lock became stale, operators had no built-in visibility or guided repair path.

## Fix
The gateway lock module now exposes a small inspection helper that reports pid liveness, age, stale reasons, and optional stale-file removal. `doctor` calls a new `noteGatewayLockHealth` helper so stale gateway locks appear in normal doctor output and are automatically removed when the operator runs `openclaw doctor --fix`.

## Validation
- `pnpm exec vitest run "src/commands/doctor-gateway-locks.test.ts" "src/commands/doctor-session-locks.test.ts" "src/infra/gateway-lock.test.ts"`

target.title: fix(security): use constant-time comparison for TLS fingerprint verification
target.body:
## Summary

TLS certificate fingerprint verification in `src/gateway/client.ts` uses `!==` (standard JavaScript string equality), which is vulnerable to **timing side-channel attacks** (CWE-208).

An attacker on the network path can measure response time differences to iteratively guess the correct fingerprint byte-by-byte, potentially bypassing TLS pinning for MITM attacks on gateway connections.

## Root cause

Two locations perform fingerprint comparison with `!==`:

| Location | Context |
|----------|---------|
| Line 205 | WebSocket `checkServerIdentity` callback |
| Line 681 | HTTP `validateTlsFingerprint` method |

## Fix

Replace both with `safeEqualSecret()` from `src/security/secret-equal.ts`, which uses `SHA-256` hashing + `crypto.timingSafeEqual` for constant-time comparison.

This is the **same function already used** for pairing token verification elsewhere in the codebase — just missed for TLS fingerprints.

```diff
- if (fingerprint !== expected) {
+ if (!safeEqualSecret(fingerprint, expected)) {
```

## Why safeEqualSecret and not raw timingSafeEqual

`timingSafeEqual` requires both buffers to have equal length, which would leak the length of the expected fingerprint. `safeEqualSecret` avoids this by hashing both values with SHA-256 first (producing fixed 32-byte digests), then comparing the digests in constant time.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm tsgo` — compiles cleanly
- [x] `pnpm check` — lint/format passes
- [x] Existing `safeEqualSecret` tests in `src/security/audit-extra.sync.test.ts` cover equal, unequal, different-length, and undefined inputs
- [x] No remaining `fingerprint !== expected` patterns in codebase

## Summary

Fixes #48708 — adds an option to display timestamps on each message in the TUI chat interface.

Enable in config:

{
  "tui": {
    "showTimestamps": true
  }
}

Messages then display as:

[11:32] system message here
[11:32]
user message here
[11:33] assistant response here

- `src/tui/tui.ts`: Read `tui.showTimestamps` from config and pass to ChatLog.

target.title: fix(deepinfra): load all DeepInfra models when user wants to browse t…
target.body: # fix(deepinfra): load all DeepInfra models when user wants to browse them during onboarding ## Summary - **Problem**: DeepInfra plugin pinned the chat catalog to 7 hardcoded manifest entries (a wizard-vs-discovery guard short-circuited discovery once the auth wizard seeded models), had no live discovery seam for image-gen / video-gen, and silently dropped Anthropic `cache_control` markers when proxying `anthropic/*` models — prompt caching was advertised eligible but never fired. - **Solution**: Wire DeepInfra's `/v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta` (the agent-tagged projection now shipping in the backend) through a per-surface helper that buckets responses by short-alias tag (`chat`/`vlm`/`embed`/`image-gen`/`video-gen`/`tts`/`stt`). Register live image-gen and video-gen catalogs via `registerModelCatalogProvider` (OpenRouter pattern). Replace the OpenRouter-only Anthropic cache wrapper with a deepinfra-local one gated on the model id. - **What changed**: new `surface-model-catalogs.ts` (live image-gen + video-gen catalog providers + `resolveModelCapabilities`); new `cache-wrapper.ts` (Anthropic ephemeral cache marker injection for `anthropic/*` on DeepInfra); refactored `provider-models.ts` (per-surface bucketed discovery, API-key gated, 5-min cache, sync static fallback); media-provider builders now accept the per-surface catalog; `index.ts` feeds all media surfaces from one source + registers the new catalog providers; `media-models.ts` `DEFAULT_*` constants demoted to `*_FALLBACK_MODELS`; TTS default bumped to `Qwen/Qwen3-TTS`. - **Scope boundary**: No edits outside `extensions/deepinfra/`. No SDK changes (`src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.ts` is byte-identical to `main`). TTS/STT/VLM/embed surfaces still use static fallback only — extending `UnifiedModelCatalogKind` to cover them is a separate SDK PR. ## Motivation DeepInfra ships hundreds of models, but openclaw only surfaced 7 hardcoded chat IDs — every new model needed a code change here. The DeepInfra backend now emits surface-tagged responses on `/v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta`, so the plugin can map tags → surfaces dynamically. Anthropic prompt caching on DeepInfra was also silently broken: the runner advertised eligibility but the OpenRouter-only stream wrapper short-circuited on `provider !== "openrouter"`, so `cache_control` markers never went out. ## Change Type - [x] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [x] Refactor required for the fix ## Scope - [x] Integrations ## Linked Issue/PR - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Real behavior proof - **Environment**: dev workstation + hosted instance (Ubuntu 24.04, `openclaw.service` via systemd, Node 22, `DEEPINFRA_API_KEY` configured). Same matrix on both. - **Steps after this patch**: ```bash pnpm build pnpm test extensions/deepinfra rm -rf ~/.openclaw-dev DEEPINFRA_API_KEY= pnpm dev --dev capability model providers | grep deepinfra DEEPINFRA_API_KEY= pnpm dev --dev capability model list | grep '"provider":"deepinfra"' | wc -l DEEPINFRA_API_KEY= pnpm dev --dev capability image providers | grep deepinfra DEEPINFRA_API_KEY= pnpm dev --dev capability audio providers | grep deepinfra ``` - **Evidence after fix**: - `capability model providers` → `{"provider":"deepinfra","count":88,"defaults":["anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5","anthropic/claude-opus-4-7","anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6"],"available":true,...}` (was: 7 on `main`). - `capability model list | grep '"provider":"deepinfra"' | wc -l` → 88 (was: 7). - `capability image providers` → deepinfra row with `FLUX-1-schnell` default + fallback allowlist. - `capability audio providers` → `{"id":"deepinfra","configured":true,"capabilities":["image","audio"],"defaultModels":{"image":"moonshotai/Kimi-K2.5","audio":"openai/whisper-large-v3-turbo"}}`. - `pnpm test extensions/deepinfra` → 59/59. - `pnpm check:changed` → exit 0 (one pre-existing unrelated lint failure in `scripts/generate-plugin-inventory-doc.mjs:398`, last touched in `85f9276624`). - **Not tested**: end-to-end TTS/STT audio round-trip and a real image/video-gen request against the live backend. The runtime providers register correctly and discovery returns the right shape; downstream inference was not exercised. ## Root Cause - (a) `augmentModelCatalog` short-circuited with `existingDeepInfraCount > DEEPINFRA_MODEL_CATALOG.length`, skipping discovery whenever the auth wizard had seeded entries first. - (b) Media surfaces had no `registerModelCatalogProvider` wiring. - (c) `wrapStreamFn` reused `createOpenRouterSystemCacheWrapper`, which short-circuits on `provider !== "openrouter"`, so Anthropic ephemeral cache markers never reached the payload for DeepInfra-routed Anthropic models even though `isCacheTtlEligible` returned `true`. **Missing guardrail**: no test asserted that `wrapStreamFn` applied `cache_control` markers for `anthropic/*` ids; no test asserted that the augmented catalog grew past the manifest size on successful discovery. ## Regression Test Plan - **Coverage level**: [x] Unit test [x] Seam / integration test - **Target tests**: - `extensions/deepinfra/cache-wrapper.test.ts` (new) — asserts `cache_control: { type: "ephemeral" }` markers are applied for `anthropic/*` ids and skipped otherwise. - `extensions/deepinfra/surface-model-catalogs.test.ts` (new) — asserts the live catalog providers return `null` without a key, return `null` on empty surface buckets, and project entries with the correct shape otherwise. - `extensions/deepinfra/provider-models.test.ts` (extended) — per-surface bucketing + manifest fallback path. - `extensions/deepinfra/index.test.ts` (extended) — augmentModelCatalog merges discovered + wizard-seeded entries instead of suppressing one. - extensions/deepinfra/cache-wrapper.test.ts (4 tests) exercises createDeepInfraAnthropicCacheWrapper against anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 & anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5 and asserts cache_control: { type: "ephemeral" } is injected into the system message under both string-content and array-content shapes; negative test confirms non-anthropic ids pass through untouched. - extensions/deepinfra/provider-models.test.ts (3 new cases) mocks openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-auth.isProviderApiKeyConfigured and asserts hasDeepInfraApiKey({ env: {}, agentDir }) falls back to the auth-profile store with the correct provider/agentDir args, short-circuits on env var, and returns false when both env and store are empty. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - **Chat catalogue**: with `DEEPINFRA_API_KEY` set, OpenClaw surfaces the full DeepInfra catalogue (88 chat + 38 vlm today across `anthropic/`, `ByteDance/`, `deepseek-ai/`, `google/`, `meta-llama/`, `Qwen/`, `zai-org/`, …). Without a key, the curated 7-model manifest fallback is served. - **Image-gen + video-gen**: a live `registerModelCatalogProvider` now refreshes per-kind catalogs from DeepInfra when a key is configured. Without a key, the curated static fallback is served. - **TTS default**: `hexgrad/Kokoro-82M` → `Qwen/Qwen3-TTS`. Users on the implicit default will now hit Qwen3-TTS; Kokoro stays in the fallback list and can be selected explicitly. - **Onboarding picker**: discovered models are no longer hidden behind wizard-seeded entries. - **Anthropic prompt caching** actually fires on DeepInfra-routed `anthropic/*` requests. Multi-turn conversations should show non-zero `cache_creation_input_tokens` on turn 1 and `cache_read_input_tokens` on turn 2+. - Backward compatible; no config/env/migration changes. ## Diagram ```text Before: [user pastes API key] -> [auth wizard seeds 7 manifest entries] -> [augmentModelCatalog: count > MODEL_CATALOG.length? -> skip discovery] -> [picker shows 7 models] [anthropic/* on DeepInfra request] -> [isCacheTtlEligible -> true] -> [createOpenRouterSystemCacheWrapper -> provider !== "openrouter" -> short-circuit] -> [payload sent WITHOUT cache_control markers, prompt cache never fires] After: [user pastes API key] -> [auth wizard seeds entries] -> [augmentModelCatalog: always run discovery] -> [discoverDeepInfraSurfaces: GET /v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta] -> [bucket by surface alias tag] -> [picker shows 88 chat + 38 vlm + ...] [anthropic/* on DeepInfra request] -> [isCacheTtlEligible -> true] -> [createDeepInfraAnthropicCacheWrapper: model.id starts with "anthropic/" -> apply patch] -> [payload sent WITH cache_control: {type: "ephemeral"}, prompt cache fires] ``` ## Security Impact - New permissions/capabilities? **No** - Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No** - New/changed network calls? **Yes** — one read-only GET to `https://api.deepinfra.com/v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta` per process per 5 minutes, gated on `DEEPINFRA_API_KEY` being configured. The endpoint is unauthenticated; the gate is a UX choice (pre-auth flows show the static fallback, no useless network traffic). No PII or secrets in the request. - Command/tool execution surface changed? **No** - Data access scope changed? **No** ## Human Verification (Observed Result) ran `pnpm dev --dev onboard` with provider deepinfra and successfully loaded all discovery models: Tested various `pnpm dev --dev capability ` commands (see section "Real behavior proof") ## Risks and Mitigations - **Risk**: production DeepInfra backend hasn't (yet) tagged any `video-gen` model with the OPENAI tag, so the `video-gen` surface bucket from live discovery is empty. **Mitigation**: the live catalog seam returns `null` on empty buckets so the static `DEEPINFRA_VIDEO_FALLBACK_MODELS` list wins. Once the backend tags T2V models, the live catalog takes over automatically — no deepclaw redeploy needed. - **Risk**: TTS default change (`Kokoro-82M` → `Qwen3-TTS`) could surprise users on the implicit default. **Mitigation**: Kokoro stays in the fallback list and can be selected explicitly; documented in CHANGELOG.

当网关离线时（如电脑睡眠），飞书消息丢失。飞书 WebSocket 不像 Telegram 会重放离线消息。

- extensions/feishu/src/feishu-message-recovery.ts - 消息恢复模块

## 功能特性
- 定期检查消息（每30秒）
- 自动恢复丢失的消息
- 消息处理统计
- 支持手动触发恢复

target.title: ACP: add gateway-owned node-backed runtime
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: OpenClaw did not yet have a complete **node-backed ACP runtime** where the gateway owns the durable state and a paired node acts only as the execution worker.
- Why it matters: without that split, restart/replay/reconnect behavior is too dependent on the live process, which makes lease fencing, terminal resolution, and outward delivery recovery harder to reason about and prove.
- What this PR adds:
  - a durable ACP gateway store for sessions, runs, accepted worker events, leases, terminal state, replay targets, and projector checkpoints
  - gateway ingestion of ACP worker events coming back from paired nodes
  - a real node-host ACP worker bridge and gateway/node command plumbing
  - a durable ACP reply projection/replay path so restart recovery can resume from gateway state instead of only from the live iterator
  - startup recovery for incomplete ACP node runs
  - restart/reconnect/cancel/close race hardening with focused restart/replay test coverage
  - architecture / protocol / verification docs for the new backend
- Landing-only follow-up: after rebasing onto current `main`, this PR also includes a few **test-only** isolation fixes in Feishu / Google / Google Chat extension tests that were needed to make the landing branch green. Those are not ACP runtime behavior changes.

## What changed by area

### 1) Durable ACP gateway state

New ACP store/state plumbing under `src/acp/store/**`:

- persists ACP sessions and runs
- persists accepted worker events
- tracks leases / lease epochs
- resolves canonical terminal state on the gateway
- stores replay targets and projector checkpoints separately from the runtime cursor

This is the foundation that lets the gateway stay authoritative even when execution happens on a paired node.

### 2) Gateway ↔ node ACP worker path

Main implementation areas:

- `src/gateway/**`
- `src/infra/node-commands.ts`
- `src/node-host/**`

This adds the node-backed ACP execution path:

- gateway can start/control ACP work on a paired node
- node-host can run the ACP worker role
- worker events stream back to the gateway over the existing node transport
- gateway remains the source of truth for run state and terminal resolution

### 3) Durable reply projection and replay

Main implementation areas:

- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-replay.ts`

This PR moves ACP outward delivery onto a durable replayable path:

- reply targets are persisted per run
- projector checkpoints are advanced only after successful delivery
- restart recovery can replay the unfinished suffix from durable state
- reconnect and duplicate accepted events do not re-deliver already completed outward effects

### 4) Startup recovery / reconnect handling

Main implementation areas:

- `src/gateway/server-startup.acp-node.ts`
- `src/gateway/server-node-events.ts`
- `src/acp/runtime/acp-node.ts`

This adds the gateway-side recovery flow needed to:

- resume incomplete projection after restart
- keep reconnect handling deterministic
- prevent stale-worker wins
- preserve canonical terminal behavior across restart/replay windows

### 5) Test harnesses and regression coverage

Main test additions/expansions:

- `src/acp/store/store.restart.test.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.replay.test.ts`
- `src/gateway/server-startup.acp-node.test.ts`
- `src/gateway/server-node-events.acp.test.ts`
- `src/node-host/invoke-acp.test.ts`
- `src/acp/test-harness/restart-harness.ts`

These cover the core failure windows this backend needs to survive:

- restart after accepted events but before replay checkpoint advancement
- restart after terminal persistence but before final outward convergence
- reconnect replay of already accepted events
- cancel / complete / close race handling
- restart recovery with a real memory cut instead of reusing in-process objects

### 6) Docs included in this PR

Main docs:

- `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-backed-runtime.md`
- `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-backed-runtime-protocol.md`
- `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-backed-runtime-verification.md`
- `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-runtime-program.md`
- `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-slice4-summary.md`
- `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-slice4-final-judge.md`

These are included because this was developed as a staged architecture / verification effort and the final accepted behavior is documented there.

## Explicit scope boundaries / v1 limitations

This PR does **not**:

- turn nodes into mini-gateways
- add automatic cross-node continuation for an in-flight run
- claim durable resumed tool-message edit support after restart

Current conservative post-restart tool-update behavior remains:

- `append_only_after_restart`

target.title: fix(plugins): resolve native plugin sdk aliases
target.body:
## Summary
- install a narrow native resolver for `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` and `@openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` when loading plugin modules
- keep native `createRequire()` SDK subpath resolution on built JS dist artifacts, even when the plugin loader prefers source aliases
- guard the resolver to parents under registered plugin roots so unrelated process `require()` calls still use Node's normal resolver
- add regression coverage for read-only dist trees, source/dist preference, and the parent-root guard

Refs #85123.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/plugin-sdk-native-resolver.test.ts`
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: built external plugin runtimes can resolve OpenClaw plugin SDK subpaths from plugin-local `createRequire()` calls without a plugin-local `node_modules/openclaw` shim, runtime writes to the OpenClaw package tree, or `NODE_PATH`.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout using a fake built OpenClaw package and fake built external plugin runtime under `src/plugins/plugin-sdk-native-resolver.test.ts`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/plugin-sdk-native-resolver.test.ts`
Evidence after fix: the regression test resolves `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-message` from the registered external plugin runtime, verifies the resolved file is `dist/plugin-sdk/channel-message.js` even when source files exist, verifies no `dist/extensions` alias tree is created in a read-only dist tree, and verifies an unrelated parent module cannot resolve the alias through this hook.
Observed result after fix: 1 test file passed, 3 tests passed.
What was not tested: no live third-party Mentat runtime; this PR intentionally covers the generic resolver half only and leaves channel behavior to the generic channel-message PR.

target.title: [AI-assisted] fix(pi-runner): flush block replies after compaction retry
target.body:
## Summary
- Fixes #47335.
- Flushes the block reply pipeline again after the compaction retry wait resolves, so retry-generated block replies cannot still be draining when the attempt returns.
- Skips the extra drain on aggregate timeout to preserve the existing timeout/slow-channel behavior.

## Verification
- `corepack pnpm exec tsx /private/tmp/openclaw-compaction-retry-delivery-proof.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts -t "flushes block replies again after compaction retry wait resolves"`
- `pnpm check:architecture`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/compaction-retry-aggregate-timeout.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-payloads.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-pi-session.splits-long-single-line-fenced-blocks-reopen.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-pi-session.waits-multiple-compaction-retries-before-resolving.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.test-support.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Compaction retry can resolve the runner wait before retry-generated block replies finish draining, causing transcript-visible replies to be dropped before channel delivery.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout at commit `70f45ff870e43fbb939c680b74821015ecdccce6`, rebased onto `origin/main` at `e2f82d4d30bbebbf89bdbb94b3e58c7aa0185151` on macOS, with dependencies installed from `pnpm-lock.yaml`.

The log-backed proof uses the real `createBlockReplyPipeline().flush()` delivery barrier and the real `waitForCompactionRetryWithAggregateTimeout()` helper. The local channel adapter deliberately delays send completion so the retry wait / delivery ordering is visible without external provider credentials.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `corepack pnpm exec tsx /private/tmp/openclaw-compaction-retry-delivery-proof.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts -t "flushes block replies again after compaction retry wait resolves"`
- `pnpm check:architecture`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/compaction-retry-aggregate-timeout.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-payloads.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-pi-session.splits-long-single-line-fenced-blocks-reopen.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-pi-session.waits-multiple-compaction-retries-before-resolving.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.test-support.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Evidence after fix:
```txt
pre-wait-flush-start
pre-wait-flush-complete
retry-wait-start
retry-generated-block-enqueued
channel-send-start:retry reply delivered after compaction wait
retry-wait-resolved
aggregate-wait-result:{"timedOut":false}
delivered-before-post-wait-flush:none
delivery-in-flight-before-post-wait-flush-completes:yes
channel-send-complete:retry reply delivered after compaction wait
post-wait-flush-complete:retry reply delivered after compaction wait
```

- Rebased head: `70f45ff870e43fbb939c680b74821015ecdccce6` on `origin/main` `e2f82d4d30bbebbf89bdbb94b3e58c7aa0185151`.
- Target regression after rebase: `Test Files 2 passed (2)`, `Tests 2 passed | 94 skipped (96)`.
- Architecture after rebase: `Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).`, `Madge import cycle check: 0 cycle(s).`, `deprecated API usage guard passed`, `deprecated JSDoc guard passed`.
- Runner/lifecycle/compaction selection: `Test Files 6 passed (6)`, `Tests 166 passed (166)`.
- Supplemental payload/subscribe selection: `Test Files 5 passed (5)`, `Tests 62 passed (62)`.
- oxfmt: `All matched files use the correct format.`
- `git diff --check` passed with no output.

Observed result after fix: The retry wait returns with the retry reply still undelivered (`delivered-before-post-wait-flush:none`), then the second idempotent flush waits for the real block reply pipeline send chain to finish (`post-wait-flush-complete:retry reply delivered after compaction wait`). Aggregate-timeout fallback still avoids an extra drain.

What was not tested: A live high-context Discord/Telegram/other external-network compaction retry with real provider credentials was not run. The behavior proof is local and log-backed, using the real block reply pipeline flush and retry wait helper with a delayed local channel adapter.

## AI Assistance Disclosure
This PR was prepared with AI assistance. I reviewed the changed code path and verified the targeted tests above.

target.title: fix(install): handle npm min-release-age in hosted installers
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `install.sh` / `install-cli.sh` could still emit `--before=<now>` when npm only exposed a computed `before` cutoff from a raw `min-release-age` npmrc entry.
- Solution: inspect raw npm config files for `min-release-age` before choosing the installer freshness bypass mode.
- What changed: shell and PowerShell installers now keep `--min-release-age=0` when a raw `min-release-age` policy is present, instead of trusting npm's computed `before` output.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): installer package selection and managed TypeScript helper runtime behavior are unchanged; the TypeScript tests were made hermetic for machines that already have npm freshness policy in user/global npmrc.

## Motivation

- Fixes the remaining hosted-installer path reported in #84743 after #82641 / #83761.
- npm 11 can report `npm config get min-release-age` as `null` while `npm config get before` returns a computed cutoff from `min-release-age=7`; using that computed cutoff as `--before=<now>` still conflicts with the raw npmrc policy.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #84743
- Related #82630
- Related #82641
- Related #83761
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: hosted installer npm calls no longer choose `--before=<now>` when a raw npmrc `min-release-age` setting is what produced npm's computed `before` cutoff.
- Real environment tested: macOS arm64, Node `v26.0.0`, npm `11.12.1`, disposable OpenClaw checkout under `/private/tmp/openclaw-installer-freshness-pr`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/install-sh.test.ts test/scripts/install-cli.test.ts test/scripts/install-ps1.test.ts src/infra/npm-install-env.test.ts`
  - `bash -n scripts/install.sh scripts/install-cli.sh`
  - `git diff --check`
  - Mixed-config regression proof: project `.npmrc` has `min-release-age=7`, user `.npmrc` has `before=2026-01-01T00:00:00.000Z`, and the installer still chooses the global-install `--before=<now>` path.
  - Real npm installer-function proof with `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_SH_NO_RUN=1`, `HOME=<tmp>` containing `.npmrc` with `min-release-age=7`, `NPM_CONFIG_PREFIX=<tmp-prefix>`, and a local no-script package to avoid network/global mutation.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/install-sh.test.ts test/scripts/install-cli.test.ts test/scripts/install-ps1.test.ts src/infra/npm-install-env.test.ts

Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests  51 passed | 4 skipped (55)
```

```text
bash -n scripts/install.sh scripts/install-cli.sh
exit status 0

git diff --check
exit status 0
```

```text
OPENCLAW_INSTALL_SH_NO_RUN=1 HOME=<tmp with .npmrc min-release-age=7> \
  NPM_CONFIG_PREFIX=<tmp-prefix> NPM_LOGLEVEL=error \
  bash -c 'source scripts/install.sh; run_npm_global_install <local no-script package> <tmp-log>'

LAST_NPM_INSTALL_CMD=env -u NPM_CONFIG_BEFORE -u npm_config_before -u NPM_CONFIG_MIN_RELEASE_AGE -u npm_config_min_release_age -u npm_config_min-release-age SHARP_IGNORE_GLOBAL_LIBVIPS=1 npm --loglevel error --silent --no-fund --no-audit --min-release-age=0 install -g <local no-script package>
exit=0
home_npmrc=min-release-age=7
```

```text
npm --version
11.12.1

HOME=<tmp with .npmrc min-release-age=7> npm config get min-release-age
null

HOME=<tmp with .npmrc min-release-age=7> npm config get before
Wed May 13 2026 21:45:21 GMT-0300 (Brasilia Standard Time)
```

- Observed result after fix: the patched `scripts/install.sh` npm path uses `--min-release-age=0`, emits no `--before=`, and real npm exits 0 under a raw user `min-release-age=7` npmrc. For a mixed project-only `min-release-age` plus user `before` policy, the patched global-install path ignores project `.npmrc`, emits `--before=<now>`, and real npm exits 0.
- What was not tested: PowerShell runtime execution; neither `pwsh` nor `powershell` is installed in this environment. `test/scripts/install-ps1.test.ts` still passed its static coverage.
- Before evidence: the new shell installer fixture tests failed before the implementation with status `64`, matching npm's `--min-release-age cannot be provided when using --before` conflict.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the shell installer freshness bypass checked npm's resolved config output instead of raw npmrc keys. npm 11 can compute `before` from `min-release-age`, so the installer mistook a release-age policy for an explicit `before` policy.
- Missing detection / guardrail: installer tests asserted strings existed but did not execute the installer npm path against a fixture that reproduces npm's computed `before` behavior.
- Contributing context (if known): the TypeScript helper already scans raw npm config files; the hosted shell installers did not have equivalent detection.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `test/scripts/install-sh.test.ts`
  - `test/scripts/install-cli.test.ts`
  - `test/scripts/install-ps1.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/npm-install-env.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: fake npm reports `min-release-age` as `null`, reports a computed `before`, and fails if the installer emits `--before=`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it exercises the installer shell function directly without hitting the network or mutating a real npm install.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): the TypeScript helper had this guardrail; the hosted installer execution path did not.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

OpenClaw hosted installers should now work for users whose npmrc contains `min-release-age`.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[npmrc min-release-age] -> [npm computes before] -> [installer emits --before] -> [npm config conflict]

After:
[npmrc min-release-age] -> [installer detects raw key] -> [installer emits --min-release-age=0] -> [npm accepts command]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A
